requirements.text: The application shall have two primary views within a conversation: Chat View (Slack-like group conversation) and Dashboard View (project management board). The user shall be able to switch between them.

tests.description: Renders Chat and Dashboard toggle buttons in TopBar
Clicking Dashboard shows KanbanBoard in center pane
Clicking Chat returns to ChatPane with messages preserved

requirements.text: The layout for switching between Chat and Dashboard is TBD (tabs, split pane, sidebar, etc.) and shall be determined during design phase.

tests.description: Renders Chat and Dashboard toggle buttons in TopBar
Clicking Dashboard shows KanbanBoard in center pane
Clicking Chat returns to ChatPane with messages preserved

requirements.text: The application shall be responsive and usable on desktop browsers. LAN access from phone/tablet is a secondary use case.

tests.description: Four-zone layout renders correctly at 1280px viewport width
Sidebar collapses to icon-only at <1024px viewport
No horizontal overflow at 768px viewport width

requirements.text: The chat view shall resemble a Slack channel. Each message shows: agent name (or "You" for the customer), avatar/icon, timestamp, and message body.

tests.description: Renders agent message with avatar showing first letter and hash-based color
Renders markdown content (code block, bold, link) via react-markdown
Renders phase_change type as a styled divider with phase label
Renders steer message with highlight border and 'You' sender label
Fetches initial messages from API on mount
Appends new messages from SSE stream in real-time
Deduplicates messages by ID when SSE delivers already-fetched messages

requirements.text: Messages from different agents shall be visually distinguishable (unique colors, icons, or badges per agent).

tests.description: Renders agent message with avatar showing first letter and hash-based color
Renders markdown content (code block, bold, link) via react-markdown
Renders phase_change type as a styled divider with phase label
Renders steer message with highlight border and 'You' sender label

requirements.text: The chat shall support markdown rendering in messages (code blocks, lists, bold, links, etc.).

tests.description: Renders agent message with avatar showing first letter and hash-based color
Renders markdown content (code block, bold, link) via react-markdown
Renders phase_change type as a styled divider with phase label
Renders steer message with highlight border and 'You' sender label

requirements.text: The chat shall auto-scroll to the latest message. The user shall be able to scroll back through history.

tests.description: Auto-scrolls to bottom when new message arrives and user is at bottom
Shows 'New messages' badge when user scrolls up and new message arrives
Clicking badge scrolls to latest message and hides badge
Fetches initial messages from API on mount
Appends new messages from SSE stream in real-time
Deduplicates messages by ID when SSE delivers already-fetched messages

requirements.text: The message composer shall support @ mentions. Typing @ shall show an autocomplete dropdown of agent names. Addressing a specific agent sends the message directly to that agent.

tests.description: Shows autocomplete dropdown when user types '@' with agent list
Filters dropdown as user types after '@' (e.g., '@ri' shows only 'Rick')
Selecting an agent inserts @AgentName and closes dropdown
Send with @mention calls onSend with target_agent_id; send without mention calls with broadcast

requirements.text: A message without an @ mention shall be broadcast to the group (all agents see it).

tests.description: Shows autocomplete dropdown when user types '@' with agent list
Filters dropdown as user types after '@' (e.g., '@ri' shows only 'Rick')
Selecting an agent inserts @AgentName and closes dropdown
Send with @mention calls onSend with target_agent_id; send without mention calls with broadcast

requirements.text: The composer shall support multi-line input (Shift+Enter for newline, Enter to send).

tests.description: Shows autocomplete dropdown when user types '@' with agent list
Filters dropdown as user types after '@' (e.g., '@ri' shows only 'Rick')
Selecting an agent inserts @AgentName and closes dropdown
Send with @mention calls onSend with target_agent_id; send without mention calls with broadcast

requirements.text: During debate, the chat shall show: current round number (e.g., "Round 5/20"), which agent is currently "speaking" (thinking indicator), and the current phase label.

tests.description: Renders phase name and round counter (e.g., 'Debate -- Round 5/20')
Shows speaking indicator with agent name when an agent is generating
Hides speaking indicator when no agent is speaking

requirements.text: When an agent @ mentions the customer for clarification, the system shall show a prominent notification/banner: "Agent X needs your input" with the chat scrolled to the question.

tests.description: Renders amber banner with agent name when clarification is needed
Clicking 'View Question' calls scrollToMessage callback with message ID
Banner is dismissible and does not render when no clarification is pending
Dispatches clarification notification when agent @mentions customer
Dispatches warning notification when max_rounds is reached
Dispatches info notification when PR is ready

requirements.text: The customer shall have controls to: approve and advance to next phase, steer the debate (send a message), or end the debate and decide.

tests.description: Renders Approve, Steer, and Decide buttons
Clicking Approve calls onApprove callback
Clicking Steer calls onSteer callback to focus composer
Clicking Decide calls onDecide callback

requirements.text: The system shall provide a per-agent activity viewer that shows the agent's real-time stream: thinking, file reads/writes, shell commands, tool calls, and their outputs.

tests.description: Renders with terminal aesthetic: dark background, monospace font
Renders thinking event as dimmed italic text
Renders tool_call event with highlighted tool name badge and tool_result indented below
Shows Resume button when user scrolls up; clicking it re-enables auto-scroll
Connects to SSE endpoint and receives parsed events
Reconnects with Last-Event-ID header after connection error
Closes EventSource on component unmount (cleanup)
Exposes connection status (connecting, open, closed, error)
Connects to agent activity SSE endpoint when agent ID is provided
Parses typed activity events (thinking, tool_call, error)
Disconnects and clears events when agent ID changes

requirements.text: The activity viewer shall be show/hide toggleable. Clicking an agent in the roster opens their activity stream. Closing it returns to the clean chat view.

tests.description: Renders with terminal aesthetic: dark background, monospace font
Renders thinking event as dimmed italic text
Renders tool_call event with highlighted tool name badge and tool_result indented below
Shows Resume button when user scrolls up; clicking it re-enables auto-scroll
Defaults to showing AgentRoster in right pane
Switches to ActivityViewer when an agent is selected
Back to Roster button returns to roster view

requirements.text: The activity viewer shall resemble a terminal/log view — monospace font, streaming output, auto-scroll to bottom with ability to scroll back.

tests.description: Renders with terminal aesthetic: dark background, monospace font
Renders thinking event as dimmed italic text
Renders tool_call event with highlighted tool name badge and tool_result indented below
Shows Resume button when user scrolls up; clicking it re-enables auto-scroll
Connects to agent activity SSE endpoint when agent ID is provided
Parses typed activity events (thinking, tool_call, error)
Disconnects and clears events when agent ID changes

requirements.text: Internal agent activity (tool calls, thinking) shall NEVER appear in the main chat. Only deliberate messages (debate turns, status updates, final outputs) appear in chat.

tests.description: Renders with terminal aesthetic: dark background, monospace font
Renders thinking event as dimmed italic text
Renders tool_call event with highlighted tool name badge and tool_result indented below
Shows Resume button when user scrolls up; clicking it re-enables auto-scroll

requirements.text: The dashboard shall show a task board with columns representing task status: Todo, Design, TDD, Implementing, Testing, PR Raised, In Review, Fixing Comments, Merging, Done.

tests.description: Renders all 10 status columns with correct headers
Shows task count in each column header
Empty columns have dimmed styling
Tasks render in the correct column based on status
Renders KanbanBoard, AgentStatusBar, and MergeQueue sections
Opens TaskDetailModal when a task card is clicked
Renders empty state when no tasks exist

requirements.text: Each task card on the board shall show: task title, assigned agent (with icon/color), PR link (if exists), and reviewer (if assigned).

tests.description: Renders task title, agent badge, and status accent border
Long title is truncated with tooltip showing full text
PR link renders as clickable link opening in new tab when PR exists
Clicking card calls onClick handler with task ID

requirements.text: The dashboard shall show each agent's current status: what task they are working on, or if they are idle/waiting.

tests.description: Renders all agents with name, provider, and status dot
Status dot has correct color class for each status (idle=green, running=blue, blocked=amber, offline=gray)
Shows current task title when agent has an assigned task
Clicking an agent row calls onSelectAgent with agent ID
Renders KanbanBoard, AgentStatusBar, and MergeQueue sections
Opens TaskDetailModal when a task card is clicked
Renders empty state when no tasks exist

requirements.text: The dashboard shall show the merge queue: ordered list of PRs waiting to merge, which one is currently merging.

tests.description: Renders merge queue entries in order with position, PR number, and task title
Currently-merging PR has highlighted styling
Shows 'No PRs in queue' when list is empty
Renders KanbanBoard, AgentStatusBar, and MergeQueue sections
Opens TaskDetailModal when a task card is clicked
Renders empty state when no tasks exist

requirements.text: Clicking on a task card shall show task details: description, scope, dependencies, agent activity log, PR status.

tests.description: Renders task title, status badge, and priority in header
Renders markdown description with code blocks and lists
Shows dependencies with their current status indicators
Closes modal when overlay is clicked or X button is pressed

requirements.text: The agent roster shall show all agents in the current conversation with: name, role (Analyst/Team Lead/Developer), provider (Claude/Codex/Ollama), status indicator (idle/working/reviewing/waiting), and current task (if any).

tests.description: Agent card shows role badge, provider icon, status dot, and current task
Clicking agent card calls onSelectAgent to open activity viewer
Back to Roster button calls onBackToRoster callback

requirements.text: Clicking an agent in the roster shall open their activity viewer.

tests.description: Agent card shows role badge, provider icon, status dot, and current task
Clicking agent card calls onSelectAgent to open activity viewer
Back to Roster button calls onBackToRoster callback
Defaults to showing AgentRoster in right pane
Switches to ActivityViewer when an agent is selected
Back to Roster button returns to roster view

requirements.text: The roster shall support adding/removing agents from a conversation. Adding requires: name, role, provider, model selection.

tests.description: Add agent form renders name, role, provider, and model fields
Form validates that name and model are required before submit
Submitting calls onSave with form data; cancel calls onCancel

requirements.text: The user shall be able to create a new conversation by specifying: title and project directory path.

tests.description: Renders conversations sorted by updated_at descending
Active conversation has highlighted styling
Clicking create opens dialog; submitting creates conversation
Right-click shows context menu with delete option

requirements.text: The user shall be able to switch between conversations. A conversation list (sidebar) shows all conversations with title, last activity time, and current phase.

tests.description: Renders conversations sorted by updated_at descending
Active conversation has highlighted styling
Clicking create opens dialog; submitting creates conversation
Right-click shows context menu with delete option

requirements.text: Conversations shall be resumable — the user can close the browser and return later. State is persisted.

tests.description: Renders conversations sorted by updated_at descending
Active conversation has highlighted styling
Clicking create opens dialog; submitting creates conversation
Right-click shows context menu with delete option

requirements.text: The system shall notify the customer when: an agent @ mentions them, debate reaches max rounds without agreement, a PR is ready for the customer's attention, or an agent is blocked/needs input.

tests.description: Renders toast with correct color styling based on notification type
Toast auto-dismisses after 8 seconds
Clicking toast calls onClick handler for navigation
Multiple toasts stack vertically in top-right corner
Dispatches clarification notification when agent @mentions customer
Dispatches warning notification when max_rounds is reached
Dispatches info notification when PR is ready

requirements.text: Notifications shall be visible in the UI (banner, badge, or toast). Browser notifications (optional, P2).

tests.description: Renders toast with correct color styling based on notification type
Toast auto-dismisses after 8 seconds
Clicking toast calls onClick handler for navigation
Multiple toasts stack vertically in top-right corner
